action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 25 eval returned 0.2499 AP with batch_size=2048 and 32 TTA runs per model. The absolute best test AP remains 0.2533 from iteration 21 (batch_size=1024, same TTA). Only ~3.2 minutes remain on the absolute 6h clock, making another full training run impossible. Analysis: Inference time scales roughly inversely with batch size. Doubling batch size to 4096 should cut wall-clock time enough to fit within the remaining ~200s while maintaining the same TTA configuration. If it finishes, stochastic TTA variance might even produce a score near the 0.2533 best. Decision: Update predict.py to batch_size=4096, keep all other logic identical, and immediately submit eval. If time expires, the existing best score of 0.2533 is preserved.
